name: Yakitori Ando
tagline: Tiny Kaimuki izakaya serving omakase-only yakitori over binchotan charcoal — Oahu's best-kept dining secret.
parking: Street parking on Center St and Waialae Ave
talkStory: With only about 10 counter seats, Chef Ando grills each skewer over imported binchotan charcoal with Tokyo-level precision — chicken hearts, tsukune with runny egg yolk, seasonal specials. The omakase-only format means you sit back and trust the chef through about 15 courses over 90 minutes. Book 3-4 weeks in advance; locals consider this Oahu's best-kept dining secret. Quietly earned one of the highest per-seat reputations in the state.
